fix(jobs): backfill empty descriptions + accept descriptionByLocale in build filter

Three fixes for the Colin & Cie deploy blocker (3 jobs × 4 locales = 12 missing JobPosting):

1. Build plugin: accept jobs with descriptionByLocale even if description is empty
2. Assemble script: backfill empty description from descriptionByLocale.it
3. By-crawler data: fix 3 Colin & Cie jobs with empty description field

Root cause: crawlers running with skip_ai_translation=1 write jobs with
empty description but populated descriptionByLocale. The build plugin's
validJobs filter required truthy description, excluding these jobs entirely.

Also includes: per-locale previousSlugs cleanup + crawler template improvements.

Add local-runner for extraction testing and improve test loading (#74)

## Fixture-based local testing with MockServer

Replaces the old test runner (which required real DevRev credentials)
with a fixture-based system that uses the SDK's `MockServer`. Snap-in
functions can now be tested locally without any external dependencies.

### Changes

**`code/src/test-runner/test-runner.ts`** -- Full rewrite

- Starts a `MockServer` (from `@devrev/ts-adaas`) on a dynamic port
- Loads `state.json` from the fixture directory and injects it via the
`GET /worker_data_url.get` route override
- Loads `event.json` and builds a complete `AirdropEvent` with all SDK
URLs (`callback_url`, `worker_data_url`, `devrev_endpoint`) pointing at
the MockServer
- All other DevRev API endpoints return default `200 OK` responses
automatically
- Supports `${ENV_VAR}` templating in fixture JSON files -- variables
are resolved from `process.env` (with `.env` auto-loaded via dotenv).
Missing variables produce a clear error message with the variable name
and file path.
- Auto-infers `functionName` from `event_type` when not provided on the
CLI

**`code/src/main.ts`** -- Updated CLI interface

- `--fixturePath` (required): name of the fixture folder inside
`code/fixtures/`
- `--functionName` (optional): which function to run. When omitted,
inferred from `event_type` in `event.json`.

**`code/package.json`**

- Added `"fixture"` npm script

**`code/.env.example`**

- Added example `.env` file with guidance on where to store environment
variables used by fixture tests

**`code/fixtures/`** -- Example fixtures

- `start_extracting_external_sync_units/` -- event.json (no state.json)
- `start_extracting_data/` -- event.json + state.json

**`code/scripts/deploy.sh`** -- Deleted

### Usage

```bash
# Run a fixture (JSON log output):
npm run start -- --fixturePath start_extracting_external_sync_units

# Run with plain-text logs:
npm run start -- --fixturePath start_extracting_external_sync_units

# Override function name from CLI:
npm run start -- --fixturePath start_extracting_data --functionName extraction
```

### Creating new fixtures

Create a folder in `code/fixtures/<name>/` with two files:

**`event.json`** (only `event_type` is required):

```json
{
  "event_type": "START_EXTRACTING_DATA",
  "connection_data": {
    "key": "${EXTERNAL_SYSTEM_TOKEN}",
    "key_type": "pat"
  }
}
```

**`state.json`** (optional -- defaults to empty state if missing):

```json
{
  "todos": { "completed": false },
  "users": { "completed": false },
  "attachments": { "completed": false }
}
```

Environment variables can be provided via a `.env` file or exported in
your shell.

### How it works

1. `dotenv.config()` loads `.env` into `process.env`
2. Fixture JSON files are read and `${VAR}` placeholders are resolved
3. `MockServer` starts on a random available port
4. `state.json` content is injected into the mock `GET
/worker_data_url.get` endpoint
5. A full `AirdropEvent` is constructed with test defaults and
MockServer URLs
6. The snap-in function is called with the event
7. MockServer is stopped after completion

No real credentials or network access to DevRev is needed. External
system calls (e.g. the `HttpClient` in the template) still hit their
configured endpoints as normal.

Connected Issues
- [#ISS-269670](https://app.devrev.ai/devrev/works/ISS-269670)

Checklist

- [x] Tests added/updated and ran with npm run test OR no tests needed.
- [x] Code formatted and checked with npm run lint.
- [x] Added "How to test" section to the description OR this section is
not needed.

Requirements
This PR has to be merged after [this
one](https://github.com/devrev/adaas-sdk/pull/178), otherwise it'll
complain about a missing `createMockEvent` function.

---------

commit.message:
Hero design refresh: diagonal pattern, subtle Konami deco, choreographed entrance

1. Break the grid above the fold: Added a diagonal SVG architectural
   pattern (hero-pattern) positioned to extend from the hero section
   into the first row of cards. Uses radial gradient mask for fade,
   with subtle intersection nodes. Hidden at mobile widths.

2. Rethink Konami glyph treatment: Replaced inline debug-looking
   glyph text with a vertical decorative element (hero-konami-deco)
   running down the left margin at 10% opacity. Removed the old
   hero-kicker::before rule. Konami easter egg overlay unaffected.

3. Signature page-load animation: Hero elements now animate with a
   choreographed entrance sequence triggered immediately (not on
   scroll): kicker slides from left (0ms), Konami deco fades (200ms),
   diagonal pattern fades (150ms), headline slides up 32px (300ms),
   description slides up (500ms). Grid cards still cascade via
   IntersectionObserver. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.

commit.message:
feat: stable YouTube streaming with cross-platform mic input (#16)

* feat: stable YouTube streaming with cross-platform mic input

- Replace frame-rate-limiting FramePacer with a dedicated StreamingThread
  that uses coarse sleep + spin-wait for sub-millisecond timing accuracy,
  decoupling display loop speed from stream delivery entirely
- Add AudioCapture class: captures system default mic via sounddevice and
  serves raw PCM over a local TCP socket that ffmpeg reads from — works on
  Windows, macOS, and Linux without hardcoding any device names
- Fall back to platform-native audio (avfoundation/pulse/dshow) if
  sounddevice is not installed, with a clear install message
- Add -tune zerolatency to cut encoder-side latency to near zero
- Add -thread_queue_size 512 and -rtbufsize 100M to prevent silent audio
  packet drops when mixing pipe video with live audio capture
- Fix Python-level pipe buffering (bufsize=0 + explicit flush) that was
  causing frames to arrive at ffmpeg in large bursts
- Add -flvflags no_duration_filesize to avoid FLV metadata buffering
- Reduce bufsize to 2x bitrate for tighter CBR (was 6x)

cost projection customization, step sizing, convergence, backward
compatibility, determinism, categorical discretization, and factory pattern.

commit.message:
security: fix AV33 pool self-award in validate_pool_distribution

Add Step 3b winner identity check to validate_pool_distribution:
- Track first wallet paid per tier in tier_winner HashMap
- Compute fairness_map from on-chain blocks_without_reward history
  (deterministic across all validators, no gossip dependency)
- After amount tolerance check, verify the paid wallet matches the
  expected fairness-rotation winner (highest blocks_without_reward/10,
  address-ascending tie-break)
- Bitmap drift guard: only enforce when actual winner appears in our
  decoded bitmap nodes, preventing false positives from Free-tier churn
- UTXO-enforcement redirect accepted when collateral_utxo_tier_map
  confirms the actual payout address belongs to the correct tier
- Tied candidates (same fairness_bonus) are all accepted as valid

Without this check a modified producer could self-award the Silver,
Bronze, or Gold pool every block. All validator nodes accepted the
block because the total amount per tier was correct. Confirmed active
exploitation observed on mainnet (blocks 1314, 1317-1325).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable surrogate pair range tests in character classes (#149)

* Add \\uhhhh unicode escape support to parser

Parse \\uhhhh (exactly 4 hex digits) in regex patterns, matching JDK
behavior. When a high surrogate is followed by a \\uhhhh low surrogate,
they are combined into a single supplementary code point.

Fixes #133

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inline flags (?d) and (?u); fix (?U) to mean UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS

Add (?d) for UNIX_LINES and (?u) for UNICODE_CASE inline flags.
Remap (?U) from RE2's NON_GREEDY to JDK's UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS,
which enables both UNICODE_GROUPS and UNICODE_CHAR_CLASS.

Fixes #134

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nested repetition limit for alternation branches

countRepeat was compounding repeat counts across alternation branches
sequentially, treating them as if all branches were visited. For
ALTERNATE nodes, only one branch is taken at match time, so only the
worst-case (most expensive) branch should be considered.

Fixes #135

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make Unicode property name lookup loose per UTS#18

Property name lookups now ignore case, underscores, hyphens, and spaces,
matching JDK behavior and UTS#18 requirements. Both binary properties
and script/category names use the same normalization. For example,
\p{IsWhiteSpace} now matches \p{IsWhite_Space}.

Fixes #136

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Accept \b{g} grapheme cluster boundary syntax

Parse \b{g} without error for JDK compatibility. Currently approximated
as an empty match (matches at every position) since full UAX#29 boundary
detection is not yet implemented.

Fixes #137

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix three-digit octal escape \0nnn parsing

The \0 escape now consumes up to three octal digits (max value 0377),
matching JDK behavior. Previously only two digits were consumed, so
\0101 was parsed as \010 + literal '1' instead of octal 101 (= 'A').

Fixes #138

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement character class intersection/subtraction (&&)

Add support for the && operator inside character classes, matching JDK
behavior. [a-z&&[def]] now correctly matches only d/e/f, and
[a-z&&[^bc]] correctly excludes b and c.

Added CharClassBuilder.intersect() for computing the intersection of
two character classes using a merge of sorted range sets.

Fixes #139

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enable surrogate pair range tests in character classes

The \uhhhh surrogate pair combination added in #133 already handles
surrogate pair ranges in character classes correctly. The \u escape
parser combines high+low surrogate pairs into supplementary code points,
so [\uD800\uDC00-\uDBFF\uDFFF] is correctly parsed as [U+10000-U+10FFFF].

Fixes #140

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
